title: Polestar reports Q1 2026 select financial and operational results
text: Polestar reported Q1 2026 select financial and operational results showing mixed performance. Revenue of $633 million remained flat year-over-year despite 7% volume growth to 13,126 retail sales. Net loss of $383 million reflected deteriorated profitability. The company attributed margin compression to increased tariff impacts on parts costs, competitive pricing pressure in premium EV markets, and currency headwinds. Adjusted EBITDA loss remained significant at approximately $300 million for the quarter. The company's strategic initiatives include expanding retail locations from approximately 150 to 250 by year-end 2026, consolidating regional manufacturing footprint, and reducing staff by roughly 25% to approximately 1,700 employees. Cash position of $676 million provides liquidity for operations and new model launches. Management reaffirmed guidance of low double-digit volume growth in 2026 and outlined sequential revenue growth trajectory through the year. Profitability improvement remains dependent on successful product mix shift toward higher-margin Polestar 3 and 4 models, tariff policy changes, and achievement of manufacturing cost efficiencies.
